---
title: "Concepts"
subtitle: "Plain-language explanations of the objects, choices, and safeguards behind extraction."
sidebar: concepts
---

The concept pages answer the questions that arise between “I have a stimulus”
and “I have features ready for analysis.” They explain why the library keeps
certain information, when it asks you to make a choice, and what it will not
guess on your behalf.

## Choose a page by question

| Your question | Start here |
|---|---|
| How do I give an image, video, recording, or passage of text to the library? | [Stimuli](stimuli.qmd) |
| Why are some outputs matrices, some events, and some tracked objects? | [Feature objects](feature-objects.qmd) |
| What happens from extraction through alignment and table export? | [Unified workflow](unified-workflow.qmd) |
| How can I see what will run before I run it? | [Catalogue and planning](catalog-and-planning.qmd) |
| How do I choose among the available feature IDs? | [Feature catalogue](feature-catalogue.qmd) |
| What happens when an optional model or program is missing? | [Execution modes](execution-modes.qmd) |
| What is a recipe, and why does it have a graph? | [Recipes and execution graphs](recipes-and-dags.qmd) |
| When should I turn feature objects into pandas tables? | [Table interchange](table-interchange.qmd) |
| How can features sampled at different rates keep their original timing? | [Native time and clocks](native-time-and-clocks.qmd) |
| How do the older fMRI window helpers relate feature time to scan time? | [fMRI windowing](fmri-windowing.qmd) |
| How do I process a directory of short WAV files as one batch? | [Audio batch workflows](audio-batch-workflows.qmd) |

## A useful reading order

If you are new to the package, read [Stimuli](stimuli.qmd), [Feature
objects](feature-objects.qmd), and [Unified workflow](unified-workflow.qmd)
first. Read [Native time and clocks](native-time-and-clocks.qmd) before
combining features that were sampled at different rates or recorded on
different clocks. The remaining pages explain particular workflow choices.
